matches.x: cease to blow towards the promised land while they were upon the waters" (Ether 6:4-5, 8). Hugh Nibley has chronicled many "ancient" accounts of tremendous winds during the fall of Babel (Lehi 177-78). In the ancient world, wind was often seen as Jehovah's "instrument in overcoming chaos (Gen. 1:2; 8:1), and in transporting a prophet (1 Kings 18:12; 2 Kings 2:16; cf. 2:11; Ezek. 8:3; 11:1)"…

the point that judgment must come, destruction results; but it comes because of the removal of God's Spirit, prophets, and restraining hand. Nephi affirmed that he has seen the destruction of his people and, therefore, knew why they would be…

My message is a witness and warning about some of the evils which threaten America—a land I love with all my heart. There are other countries with this same problem. You who have seen these dangers in the land you love…

Great are the promises concerning this land of America. We are told unequivocally that it “is a choice land, and whatsoever nation shall possess it shall be free from bondage, and from captivity, and from all other nations under heaven, if they will but serve the God of the land, who is Jesus Christ” (Ether 2:12). This is the crux of the entire matter—obedience to the commandments of God. (Gordon…

This verse can have several experiences lumped into one verse. 1) four years pass 2) Lord comes to the brother of Jared 3) the Lord stood in a cloud 4) talked with him 5) talked with him for three hours 6) chastened him for not calling upon Him. This verse does not necessarily say that the borther of Jared hadn't prayed for four years.

“And the Lord said . . . thou shalt make a hole in the top thereof, and also in the bottom thereof; and when thou shalt suffer for air, thou shalt unstop the hole thereof, and receive air. And if it so that the water come in upon thee, behold, ye shall stop the hole thereof, that ye may not perish in the flood." (p. 542, 1st Ed.). An exacting editor by removing those very significant "thereof's"…

And Noah did according to all that the Lord had commanded him.
And Noah did according to all that the Lord had instructed him.
33 And Noah did ‘according to all that the Lord commanded him. And Noah was six hundred ‘years old when the flood of waters was upon the earth.
Noah did according to all that the Lord had commanded him. Although Noah had not yet seen any actual flooding, he still entered the ark in obedience with God’s command, as he believed His word.

"Canst thou lift up thy voice to the clouds, that abundance of waters may cover thee? (Job 38:33-34.) Job insisted that he was not wise enough to answer these questions. He said to the Lord: "I am vile; what shall I answer thee? I will lay mine hand upon my mouth. Once have I spoken; but I will not answer: yea, twice; but I will proceed no further." (Job 40:4-5.) “The Lord explained why it would be necessary to build such peculiar vessels: because he was about to loose winds of incredible violence that would make the crossing a frightful ordeal at best: any windows, he warns, will be dashed to pieces; fire will be out of the question; ‘ye shall be as a whale in the midst of the sea; for the mountain waves shall dash upon you…This was no normal crossing…
